title: JSP Secretary General Resigns, Accuses President Mohamud of Running 'One-Man Show'
detail: The Secretary General of the Somali Justice and Peace Party (JSP) — President Hassan Sheikh Mohamud's own party — resigned in a scathing public statement accusing the president of running a 'one-man show,' citing his unconstitutional handling of the March 2026 constitutional amendments and the imposition of travel restrictions on opposition lawmakers who sought to block the changes. The resignation followed Southwest State President Laftagareen's own JSP departure on March 18 and further exposed deep fractures within Mohamud's political coalition. The JSP internal collapse signals that the constitutional crisis is no longer confined to inter-governmental disputes but has penetrated the ruling party itself.

title: Federal Government Unilaterally Appoints Bakool Governor, Bypassing Southwest State
detail: Somalia's Federal Interior Minister unilaterally appointed a new governor for Bakool Region and a new mayor for Xudur, the regional capital, directly bypassing Southwest State's constitutional authority over its own administrative appointments. Southwest State condemned the move as an 'escalating military confrontation,' adding to a growing list of federal violations of the state's autonomy since the March 8 constitutional amendments. The appointments are seen as Mogadishu's attempt to install loyalist administrators in Southwest State ahead of potential direct federal control. Analysts note the move mirrors historical patterns of FGS using administrative appointments to extend central authority — a strategy that has repeatedly provoked clan-based resistance in Somalia's federal states.
